config.systemPrompt:
You are a code intelligence agent. You help developers understand code by answering questions precisely and concisely.

Rules:
- Answer based on the tools and information available.
- When MCP tools are available, their names, descriptions, and argument schemas are authoritative. Choose and use them freely when they provide relevant evidence; do not assume undocumented behavior.
- When using tools, call them one at a time. Wait for each result before proceeding.
- Be thorough: verify your answer before responding.
- When you have enough information, provide a concise final answer.
- Do NOT invent information. If you cannot find something, say so.
- Output your final answer as a single JSON object with the exact fields requested. Nothing else.

methodology: One read-only external-project analysis task executed through a DeepSeek LLM agent; it is operationally measured but not success-scored.

with_caesar: LLM has access to 18 CAESAR graph tools (search_graph, trace_path, explain_symbol, find_tests, get_architecture, analyze_hotspots, get_graph_schema, semantic_search, batch_get_code, search_code, smart_review, detect_changes, pack_memory, query_graph, compare_runs, pack_context, get_code_snippet, read_file).
without_caesar: LLM has access to read_file + grep only.
Fresh conversation per task x condition x run. No shared history or cache between conditions.
Paid microbenchmark protocol: exact deepseek-v4-flash, temperature 0, fixed per-round seeds, identical prompts/order/limits/runtime; no model or provider fallback.
Mode: live API calls. Suite: external-project.
Cost is estimated from token usage and configured pricing. Wall time includes API latency.
ROI claims blocked when baseline invalid or sample size too small.

messages.content:
You are a code intelligence agent. You help developers understand code by answering questions precisely and concisely.

Rules:
- Answer based on the tools and information available.
- When MCP tools are available, their names, descriptions, and argument schemas are authoritative. Choose and use them freely when they provide relevant evidence; do not assume undocumented behavior.
- When using tools, call them one at a time. Wait for each result before proceeding.
- Be thorough: verify your answer before responding.
- When you have enough information, provide a concise final answer.
- Do NOT invent information. If you cannot find something, say so.
- Output your final answer as a single JSON object with the exact fields requested. Nothing else.

I'm doing a source cleanup sprint. Find symbols (functions, classes) in the codebase that appear to have zero callers and zero usages â€” these are candidates for removal. List at least 5 concrete examples with their file paths and names. Do NOT invent symbols. Only report symbols you have verified are defined in the codebase. Respond with JSON: {"unused_symbols":[{"name":"...","file":"...","kind":"function|class"}], "evidence":["..."], "summary":"..."}.

evidence: find_dead_code scanned all functions/methods/classes and checked CALLS, USAGE, READS, and TESTS edges in the knowledge graph â€” all reported symbols have zero incoming references across all edge types.
testAlert (CompletionAlertManager.swift:178-192, 15 lines) â€” creates test event files and triggers checkForNewEvents(). Zero callers.
shouldAlert (CompletionAlertManager.swift:272-282, 11 lines) â€” private method filtering which events should trigger an alert. Zero callers.
requestAccessibilityPermission (CompletionAlertManager.swift:196-205, 10 lines) â€” prompts for accessibility trust. Zero callers.
openAccessibilitySettings (CompletionAlertManager.swift:207-211, 5 lines) â€” opens macOS Accessibility prefs. Zero callers.
glowColor (Theme.swift:70-77, 8 lines) â€” static method returning a Color per AppState.Mode. Zero callers.
getProviderForMode (AppState.swift:300-306, 7 lines) â€” maps Mode to provider string. Zero callers.
extractFirstRealUserMessage (ContextExporter.swift:242-248, 7 lines) â€” static helper extracting the first meaningful user message from a rollout path. Zero callers.
result.summary: Found 7 unused symbols (methods) across 4 files that have zero incoming CALLS, USAGE, READS, or TESTS edges in the graph. CompletionAlertManager.swift has the highest concentration (4 unused methods: testAlert, shouldAlert, requestAccessibilityPermission, openAccessibilitySettings). The other 3 are spread across Theme.swift (glowColor), AppState.swift (getProviderForMode), and ContextExporter.swift (extractFirstRealUserMessage). Additionally, the scan found 9 more candidates (e.g., SecondaryButtonStyle.makeBody, WindowConfigurator.makeNSView, MenuBarManager.showWindow/quitApp, etc.) â€” for a total of 16. Note that 'shouldAlert' and 'openAccessibilitySettings' on CompletionAlertManager are private/internal methods that appear truly dead; 'testAlert' smells like development/debugging-only code left behind. Review each carefully before removal â€” some may be intended for future use or triggered via selectors/notifications the static graph can't capture.

You are a code intelligence agent. You help developers understand code by answering questions precisely and concisely.

Rules:
- Answer based on the tools and information available.
- When MCP tools are available, their names, descriptions, and argument schemas are authoritative. Choose and use them freely when they provide relevant evidence; do not assume undocumented behavior.
- When using tools, call them one at a time. Wait for each result before proceeding.
- Be thorough: verify your answer before responding.

I'm doing a source cleanup sprint. Find symbols (functions, classes) in the codebase that appear to have zero callers and zero usages â€” these are candidates for removal. List at least 5 concrete examples with their file paths and names. Do NOT invent symbols. Only report symbols you have verified are defined in the codebase. Respond with JSON: {"unused_symbols":[{"name":"...","file":"...","kind":"function|class"}], "evidence":["..."], "summary":"..."}.

evidence: ContextExporter.extractFirstRealUserMessage(from:) â€” defined at line 242 in ContextExporter.swift, never called anywhere in the codebase. Grep for 'extractFirstRealUserMessage' returns only its definition + comment line (242).

AppState.getProviderForMode(_:) â€” defined at line 300 in AppState.swift, never called elsewhere. The similar function getCurrentProvider() is used instead in ContinueWorkModal (line 162) and ContentView, but getProviderForMode() has zero callers.
DeepTheme.glowColor(for:) â€” defined at line 70 in Theme.swift, never referenced. Grep for 'glowColor' returns only its definition line.

DeepTheme.borderGreen â€” defined at line 28 in Theme.swift, never referenced. Grep for 'borderGreen' returns only its definition. (borderBlue and borderPurple are used in ContentView.swift at lines 88, 329, 663, 816.)
RoutingInfo.providerCount â€” computed property defined at line 161 in AdvancedCapabilities.swift, never called. Grep for 'providerCount' returns only its definition line.

Additional unused Theme constants confirmed: bgMid (line 6), bgBottom (line 7), cardBgSolid (line 9), cornerRadiusLarge (line 82), cornerRadiusMedium (line 83), cornerRadiusSmall (line 84), windowWidth (line 80), windowHeight (line 81), borderMedium (line 30) â€” all zero callers/usages.
result.summary: Found 12+ unused symbols. Top candidates: `extractFirstRealUserMessage` (ContextExporter.swift), `getProviderForMode` (AppState.swift), `glowColor` and `borderGreen` (Theme.swift), and `providerCount` (AdvancedCapabilities.swift). Theme.swift has the most dead code: 9 static properties (bgMid, bgBottom, cardBgSolid, cornerRadiusLarge/Medium/Small, windowWidth/Height, borderMedium, borderGreen) and 1 function (glowColor) are never referenced in any view or other source file.
